verdict_reasoning: BAX is a cytoplasmic pro-apoptotic protein that remains in the cytosol in healthy cells and translocates exclusively to the mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM) upon apoptotic stimulation, not to the plasma membrane. The C-terminal transmembrane domain anchors BAX to the MOM, not the cell surface. No documented surface localization or plasma-membrane-directed therapeutics (antibodies, ADCs, CAR-T, bispecifics) exist for BAX. All known BAX-targeting drugs are small-molecule intracellular/mitochondrial modulators. The protein body never reaches the extracellular-accessible outer face of the plasma membrane.
